elements.name: Description
texts: Knox describes the deserted scene on Station Island

elements.name: Source
texts: Pastoral Annals. By an Irish Clergyman [i.e. James S. Knox], pp. 384

elements.name: Publisher
texts: R. B. Seeley and W. Burnside, London

elements.name: Contributor
texts: Digitised by Google, sponsored by Princeton Library

elements.name: Text
texts: "All was as still as death, save that on one or two wave-worn rocks, sat as many old women, telling their beads with exemplary devotion. Their conceptions of piety extended to alms asking, in which exercise they appeared not to be novices; but in my instance they were rather deserving, than successful; and to the honour of their memory be it related, their demonstrations of disappointment were moderate and noiseless."
